name: atd-get-report

outputs.description: Filename (only if the report type is JSON) File.Type File type, for example "PE" (only if the report type is JSON)
outputs.name: File.Name

outputs.description: File size (only if the report type is JSON) File.MD5 MD5 hash of the file (only if the report type is JSON)
outputs.name: File.Size

outputs.description: SHA-1 hash of the file (only if the report type is JSON) File.SHA256 SHA-256 hash of the file (only if the report type is JSON)
outputs.name: File.SHA1

outputs.description: For malicious files, the vendor that made the decision File.Malicious.Description For malicious files, the reason for the vendor to make the decision
outputs.name: File.Malicious.Vendor

outputs.description: The indicator we tested (only if the report type is JSON) DBotScore.Type The type of the indicator (only if the report type is JSON)
outputs.name: DBotScore.Indicator

outputs.description: Vendor used to calculate the score (only if the report type is JSON) DBotScore.Score The actual score (only if the report type is JSON)
outputs.name: DBotScore.Vendor

outputs.description: Entry ID of the sample IP.Address IPs relevant to the sample
outputs.name: File.EntryID

outputs.description: EntryID of the report file InfoFile.Extension Extension of the report file
outputs.name: InfoFile.EntryID

outputs.description: The name of the report file, which you can use later, for example, to send the report in an email as part of a playbook task. The report name is the the taskId, for example, 7173.pdf. InfoFile.Info Information for the report file
outputs.name: InfoFile.Name

outputs.description: Size of the report file InfoFile.Type Type of the report file
outputs.name: InfoFile.Size
